kv_offload: -nkvo (KV on CPU, weights on GPU; SP hook needs host-buffer KV in v1.04 binary)
summary_findings: Ship 5,5,4,3 holds at <0.5% PPL on Qwen3-8B-Q8 — confirms v1.02 result at full ctx=4096/chunks=8 instead of the abbreviated ctx=2048/chunks=4 used in v1.03.
sqfree+spinor 5×(5,4,4,4,5) at +1.62% costs more PPL than ship at the same model — the residual+skeleton split adds error that doesn't pay back unless we want the extra compression headroom.

10-band uniform 3-bit is dramatically worse (+94%) than 5-band 5,4,4,4,5 — band-boundary artifacts dominate over per-band granularity once you cross the 4-band ship configuration. Confirms our v1.03 ctx=2048 finding was directionally correct.
10-band auto (4,4,4,4,4,3,3,3,3,3, total 35 bits) is comparable to but slightly worse than the 5-band default at fewer bits — extra bits in 10-band can't compensate for the boundary cost.
next_step: 10-band benches won't beat 5-band even at higher total bit budgets without (a) a smarter band-boundary scheme or (b) the v1.05 Vilenkin-coeff dump for sp_auto_bands so the allocator sees the actual sqfree spectrum instead of the Walsh-128 proxy.
